title: Mango Coconut Lassi
description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Poh Ling Yeow-style mango lassi: metric ingredients, exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
keywords: Poh Ling Yeow inspired Thermomix recipe
intro: This is my independent Thermomix homage to Poh Ling Yeow and the comforting appeal of mango lassi. It keeps the spirit of the original inspiration, but the method is written fresh for TM6 and TM7 cooks who want clear settings and a warm, achievable result.
ingredients: 500 g main ingredient prepared for the dish
Fresh herbs or lemon wedges, to serve
steps: Add fruit and flavourings to the mixing bowl and chop 8 sec / 0°C / speed 6.
Add yoghurt, milk or coconut milk and blend 30 sec / 0°C / speed 8 until smooth.
Taste and adjust with lemon or a little sugar if needed, then mix 5 sec / 0°C / speed 4.
Serve cold over ice immediately; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
tips: Read the full method before starting so the Thermomix settings flow smoothly.
Keep the measuring cup off when reducing or sautéing so steam can escape.
Taste at the end and adjust salt, lemon or herbs to suit your ingredients.

faq.q: Is this the original Poh Ling Yeow recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Poh Ling Yeow and mango lassi; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.

faq.q: Can I make this Mango Coconut Lassi in a TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The method is written with TM6 and TM7 settings, using metric weights, clear times, temperatures and speeds.
Yes. Most components can be prepared earlier the same day, then reheated or finished just before serving for the best texture.
